name: ptouch-cradle
description: Two-part desktop cradle + label-catch tray for Brother PT-P750W label printer. Round 7 simplifies the tray's front wall: the round-6 variable-height profile (corners at z=18, lowered center at z=10, transition arcs) is collapsed to a single uniform height z=10 across the full width. ONE concave fillet sweep (r=20) per side handles the entire side-wall (z=30) to front-wall (z=10) transition — no intermediate flat sections, no sharp points. The grab feature is now the entire low front lip — user reaches over the 10 mm front wall anywhere along its width. Top-edge fillet (r=2 on back/sides; r=0.8 on front wall — front wall is 1.6 mm thick, r=2 collapses) wraps continuously. Cradle is unchanged from round 5.

params.render_with_host_note: Top-level OpenSCAD parameter render_with_host (default false). When true, host_object_proxy() draws a 78x152x143 box at the printer's installed position inside the pocket. STL exports MUST keep render_with_host=false (proxy excluded from print geometry); use-state PNG renders override via --param render_with_host=true.
params.fillet_schedule_note: Two tiers + ONE concave side fillet on the tray front-wall transition. r=3 utility on tray vertical edge fillets and break-edges. r=10 hero on cradle exterior corners (4x), base plate corners (4x top + 4x bottom), printer-section→shelf concave fillet on both ±X sides. r=2 continuous fillet on the BACK + SIDE walls of the tray top edge (rolled inward). r=0.8 fillet on the front wall TOP (smaller than r=2 because the front wall is only 1.6 mm thick — r=2 would exceed the wall thickness and collapse the offset). r=20 concave fillet on the side-wall to front-wall corners of the tray (matches the 20 mm height drop from z=30 to z=10 — single quarter-arc, no intermediate sections). NO chamfers anywhere.
params.label_drop_note: PT-P750W auto-cuts tape; labels exit at z=64-79mm desk-ref, clear the uniform front wall top at z=21mm desk-ref by 43-58mm, drop forward and down into tray.
params.tape_exit_position: lower-middle of front face, confirmed from product photography
params.usb_dc_ports_face: back (above the 25 mm wall — cable runs over the top, no notch needed)
params.tray_front_wall_construction: Uniform-height profile in the X-Z plane, extruded along Y over the front-wall slab (y = ext_d - wall_t .. ext_d). Front wall reaches a single z=10 (front_wall_h) across the full width — no corners, no transitions, no variable-height sections. ONE concave quarter-arc r=20 per side at the upper outer corners of the front wall, sweeping continuously from side-wall top (z=30) to front-wall top (z=10) over the 20 mm height drop. Single curve, single radius, no intermediate flat sections. The arcs are concave-from-above (wall material bulges up into the cut region; user perceives a soft scoop). The grab feature is the entire low front lip — user reaches over anywhere along its width.
params.ramp_construction: Quadratic curve z(y) = 1.6 + 8.4 * ((y - 62.6) / 30)^2 connecting (62.6, 1.6) to (92.6, 10). Tangent to flat floor at the back (slope=0 at y=62.6). Concave from cavity side — curve dips below the chord; from a finger sliding forward, slope is gentle near the back, steepens monotonically toward the front (like climbing a slide). Round-6 modeling discovered that a circular arc with these endpoints + concave-from-cavity + above-floor constraint has no real solution (chord rise too small relative to chord length to keep the arc above z=floor_t). Quadratic curve satisfies all three properties simultaneously and meets the modeler-notes-v6 spirit exactly. Self-supporting in face-up print orientation (always angles up-and-forward, never overhangs).
params.user_orientation_note: +Y = user-front (tray slides out here, printer faces user). -Y = user-back (against-wall; cable runs over the top of the 25 mm wall, no notch). Render in user-frame per id/brief.md hero_views. PRIMARY hero is the use-state render with the printer proxy installed — the 'is the cradle quiet next to the printer?' check runs on cradle-user-front-in-use.png.
params.print_orientation_cradle: Base down, walls vertical (functional/use orientation). Base plate sits flush on bed. No supports needed — all features under 45° overhang.
params.print_orientation_tray: Face up (open top up), back on bed. Variable-height front wall, side fillets, and concave parabolic ramp are all self-supporting. No supports.

testPrintCandidates.reason: Tray exterior (103.2 mm W × 94.2 mm D × 30 mm H) must slide smoothly into cradle slot with 0.35 mm per-side clearance.

testPrintCandidates.feature: tray uniform low front wall + r=20 side fillet sweep
testPrintCandidates.reason: Round-7 simplification. Front wall is uniform z=10 across the full width (no variable-height profile, no corner bars). ONE concave quarter-arc r=20 per side sweeps continuously from side-wall top (z=30) to front-wall top (z=10). Verify the front-wall top reads as a single horizontal band, the side fillets are smooth concave scoops (no kinks), and there are NO sharp points or feature intersections in the front wall geometry from any angle.

Round-6 re-orientation. Quadratic curve from (y=62.6, z=1.6) to (y=92.6, z=10), tangent to flat floor at the back. Verify the surface reads as a smooth gradual incline that steepens toward the front (no humps), is concave from inside the cavity, and prints without supports.

testPrintCandidates.feature: tray top-edge fillets (r=2 back/sides, r=0.8 front)
testPrintCandidates.reason: Continuous quarter-arc roll around the tray's back + side wall tops at z=ext_h=30 (r=2). Smaller r=0.8 roll on the front wall top at z=10 because the front wall is only 1.6 mm thick (r=2 would exceed half the wall thickness and collapse the offset). Verify both fillets render smoothly and the transitions blend cleanly into the side fillet sweep.
Round-4 carry-over: slot side walls at 3.05 mm matching wall_thickness=3 mm. Verify the wrap reads as continuous in cradle-user-top-threequarter render.

Round-5 carry-over: cradle $fn=200 and slab-stack steps=64 so each slab is ~0.047 mm tall. The fillet should read as a true continuous quarter-circle in close-up renders, no stair-stepping visible at any zoom.
Generous corner radius on a 25 mm low wall — confirm it reads as 'quiet soft footprint' rather than 'bulbous'.
Quarter-arc concave sweep on ±X sides of the cradle at the printer-section→shelf transition.

Tray walls at 1.6 mm (4 perimeters at 0.4 mm) are above minimum but thin. Verify wall integrity, particularly across the front wall and the side fillet sweep regions where the wall remains 1.6 mm thick throughout.
